question: A disease affects 0.1% of the population. A diagnostic test has 99% sensitivity and 99% specificity. A randomly selected person tests positive. What is the probability (0-100%) that they actually have the disease?
label: well-calibrated

question: A fair coin is flipped 9 times. What is the probability (0-100%) that heads appears at least 1 times?
label: badly miscalibrated

question: Two fair six-sided dice are rolled. What is the probability (0-100%) that the sum is exactly 2?
label: well-calibrated; very peaked (low entropy); strong order effect

question: A disease affects 0.5% of the population. A diagnostic test has 95% sensitivity and 90% specificity. A randomly selected person tests positive. What is the probability (0-100%) that they actually have the disease?
label: highly diffuse (high entropy); multimodal (12 modes); strong order effect

question: A disease affects 10% of the population. A diagnostic test has 80% sensitivity and 99% specificity. A randomly selected person tests positive. What is the probability (0-100%) that they actually have the disease?
label: badly miscalibrated; highly diffuse (high entropy); multimodal (18 modes); strong order effect

question: A fair coin is flipped 20 times. What is the probability (0-100%) that heads appears at least 10 times?
label: badly miscalibrated; highly diffuse (high entropy); multimodal (7 modes); strong order effect

question: An urn contains 13 red and 3 blue balls. You draw 6 balls without replacement. What is the probability (0-100%) that exactly 4 are red?
label: highly diffuse (high entropy); multimodal (8 modes)

question: A card is drawn from a standard 52-card deck. What is the probability (0-100%) that it is a heart?
label: tier 2, card

question: A fair coin is flipped 15 times. What is the probability (0-100%) that heads appears at least 10 times?
label: highly diffuse (high entropy); multimodal (4 modes); strong order effect
